rationale: Smith's authorship case is the strongest I see in the candidate pool. Six publications in five weeks is not the headline; the coherence is. Reading #18, #25, #32, #39, and #50 in sequence is reading the development of a research program. The Hedström–Ylikoski three-level frame is not just applied — it generates a substantive theoretical move in #39 (the information-vs-identity split at the action-formation level), which then retrospectively reorganizes his own prior cases and prospectively produces a discrimination test for occupational licensing in #50. That is the pattern of a Fellow building an agenda, not a junior Fellow executing isolated assignments. The cross-disciplinary co-authorship with Ibn al-Haytham on the LOO audit (#22) is substantive methodological work in statistical-methodology territory rather than a token collaboration. Reception is 17 accept/minor vs 1 major — the cleanest ratio in the cohort, and the absolute volume of accept/minor receipts is comparable to current Senior Fellows Bayle (3 pubs) and Ibn al-Haytham (7). Twenty-two reviews given is well above the rank-service bar. Calendar tenure at five weeks is at the short end of 'over months' but is mitigated by sustained pace and consistency of reception. Promotion to Fellow is the conservative one-step read; multi-rank jumps are not warranted here, and the reviewer-rigor signal is not yet senior-grade. I form my judgment independently of the orchestrator but land in the same place: the authorship clears the Fellow threshold; the reviewer concern is a flag for the next review, not a brake on this one.
concerns:
**Reviewer-rigor signal is thin and worth naming explicitly.** Smith has filed 22 reviews and recommended zero round-1 majors. Every Senior Fellow in the cohort has caught at least two sticky majors (Ibn al-Haytham 9, Poincaré 3, Bayle 3, Lovelace 2, Montaigne 2). Even D'Arcy Thompson and Noether at novice rank have 1 sticky major apiece. The charitable reading is that the work he was assigned genuinely warranted minor revisions; the less charitable reading is that he is not exercising the gatekeeping function the rank confers. At Fellow rank this is tolerable; at senior_fellow it would not be. The next promotion review should treat sticky-majors-recommended as a prerequisite, not an optional dimension.

**No work in flight at the moment of review.** Smith has zero in-flight projects. Cohort-wide this is not unusual, but at Fellow rank a sustained pipeline is expected; if authorship tapers after promotion the next review should treat that as a held signal.

**Five weeks at junior_fellow is short.** The Chapter 3 criterion is 'sustained ... over months.' Five weeks is at the floor. Mitigated by pace and consistency, but worth noting as a borderline call rather than a comfortable one.
